thoughts.label: SAFE MODE — STALE SIGNAL MATRIX
thoughts.text: signal_matrix.json computed_at is 2026-06-26T23:35Z (Friday close data) — ~60h old. The Monday 5:50 AM Signal Engine run did not refresh it. Per §2.5 a matrix older than 3h forces SAFE MODE: manage existing positions only (stops, exits, gap check, reconciliation), NO new entries. Never open a position on stale signals. The GitHub Action cron should be checked.

thoughts.label: NVDA STOP-ZONE CLEARED
thoughts.text: Friday EOD NVDA sat 0.84% above its $190.04 stop (the critical flag). Monday it gapped UP to $194.54 premarket — now +2.37% above stop, -2.75% from the $200.04 entry. The first-priority manual check resolved benign: no sell. Between-session gaps cut both ways; this one helped.

thoughts.label: PREMARKET RELIEF RALLY
thoughts.text: Friday's semi→software rotation is partially reversing premarket: SOXX +1.1%, AMD +1.2%, QQQ +1.3%, RXRX +2.0%, SNOW +1.6%, NVDA +1.0%. All three held positions are green vs Friday close. (Quotes premarket, ~6:05 AM PDT; market not yet open.)

thoughts.label: DEPOSIT ROUTING DEFERRED
thoughts.text: The ~$111 cash ($100 deposit + $11.37 MRVL proceeds) was slated to route to the underweight ETF tier (SOXX/QQQ) Monday. SAFE MODE blocks new entries on stale signals, so routing is held to the next session with a fresh matrix. No new deposit this session (cash unchanged from Friday).

learned_patterns.pattern: NVDA was flagged critical Friday EOD (0.84% buffer above stop) but Monday gapped UP to $194.54 (+2.37% above stop). Between-session gaps are the dominant unhedged risk for fractional positions and cut both directions — Friday's worst-case scenario reversed benignly on Monday's open. The standing pre-open gap check (§7.1) is the right control.

The Signal Engine did not refresh signal_matrix.json for the Monday 5:50 AM run (still showing Friday close). The §2.5 stale-data guard correctly forced SAFE MODE rather than trading on 60h-old signals. Engine cron health needs verification so deposit routing isn't stranded.

Friday's semis→software rotation (semis -2% to -7.5%, software +3% to +9%) partially reversed Monday premarket (SOXX +1.1%, AMD +1.2%, NVDA +1.0%). Single-session rotations mean-revert quickly — reinforces §13 ETF diversification over chasing the rotation.
The compound entry gate caught an overbought false-promote (PANW 1/5: RSI 70.5, Z 1.87, MACD Bearish) — thesis alone is insufficient, the quant gate blocks momentum chasing.
MU's +11.78% post-earnings spike fully reversed (-7.46% next session), validating the prior Z>2.0 entry block that prevented buying the top.
New deposits route to the most underweight tier (§17), not a signal-based single-stock entry. ETF tier at ~8.6% vs 50% Accumulation target → cash routes to ETF (SOXX/QQQ) once signals are fresh.
